auth_notes: Calendly v2 uses Personal Access Tokens (API Key) for individual access or OAuth2 for application-level access. n8n supports both.
rate_limits: Standard rate limit is 30 requests per minute per user, with bursts up to 50 requests per minute.
gotchas: Webhooks and certain API features require a paid Calendly subscription (Standard plan or higher).
Calendly API v2 uses URIs (e.g., 'https://api.calendly.com/users/UUID') as identifiers rather than simple IDs, which can complicate data mapping.
Timezone data is returned in UTC; workflows must explicitly handle conversion if local time display is required for follow-up actions.
client_must_provide: Organization/User URI (if configuring custom HTTP requests)
research_notes: While n8n lists api_key as the primary auth, OAuth2 is recommended for multi-user environments. Cal.com is the primary alternative for users seeking an open-source or self-hosted scheduling solution.

effort_recommendation.rationale: While n8n lists api_key as the primary auth, OAuth2 is recommended for multi-user environments. Cal.com is the primary alternative for users seeking an open-source or self-hosted scheduling solution.
